title: Modern labs and excellent facilities
review: Modern labs, well-maintained campus and excellent sports facilities. The incubation cell is a great resource for startups.

title: Experienced professors
review: Most professors are highly experienced; some courses are research-heavy. The teaching methodology is highly practical.

title: Good rooms but average mess
review: Hostel rooms are spacious but food quality can be inconsistent. The monkeys on campus can be a nuisance.

title: Unmatched placement opportunities
review: Top MNCs visit the campus. Almost everyone who sits for placements gets a highly competitive package.

title: Vibrant and green campus
review: The campus is beautifully carved out of a national park. Cultural fests like Saarang are incredibly fun.

title: Bureaucratic processes
review: Course registration and administrative tasks can be slow and involve too much red tape.

title: Phenomenal research culture
review: Excellent funding and state-of-the-art equipment make it the best place for pursuing deep tech research.

title: Great ROI
review: Considering the brand value, education quality, and placement packages, it is absolutely worth the fees.

title: Massive placement drives
review: The sheer number of companies visiting is mind-boggling. Both mass recruiters and dream companies offer great packages.

title: Strict rules but good facilities
review: The AC premium blocks are like hotels, but the in-time rules are very strict compared to other colleges.

title: World-class infrastructure
review: Labs, library, and sports facilities are incredibly well-maintained and modern.

title: FFCS is great but exams are hectic
review: The Fully Flexible Credit System allows you to choose your timetable, but the continuous assessment tests can be exhausting.

title: Too many fines
review: Management is very strict and imposes fines for minor infractions. Bureaucracy can be annoying.

title: Riviera is an emotion
review: The clubs are very active, and Riviera is one of the biggest and best cultural fests in South India.

title: Hit or miss
review: Since there are so many faculties, finding a good one through FFCS requires talking to seniors. Some are great, others not so much.

title: Depends on your category
review: If you get in through Category 1, it's worth it. Higher categories make the ROI slightly questionable.

title: Dream packages and top roles
review: You get access to the best quantitative finance, tech, and consulting roles in the country.

title: Mood Indigo and lake views
review: Between Powai lake and the legendary Mood Indigo fest, campus life here is absolute perfection.

title: Old infrastructure in some hostels
review: While newer hostels are nice, some of the older ones have tiny rooms and need serious renovations.

title: Tough grading but great learning
review: The academic rigor is extremely high, and relative grading makes it competitive, but you learn from the best.

title: Great facilities
review: Library and sports complexes are amazing. The lecture hall complex is very modern and well-equipped.

title: Excellent lab ecosystem
review: Funding for undergraduate research is plentiful, and professors are open to collaborating on novel ideas.

title: Best ROI in India
review: The subsidized fee structure combined with tier-1 placements gives the ultimate return on investment.

title: Can be bureaucratic
review: Getting approvals for fests and club activities requires jumping through a lot of administrative hoops.

title: Strong core and IT placements
review: The college attracts top companies for both software profiles and heavy manufacturing/core engineering roles.

title: Boat club is the highlight
review: The traditional boat club is amazing. The campus has a historic vibe right in the middle of Pune.

title: Heritage buildings
review: Beautiful British-era buildings, but some labs could use modernization. Space is a bit constrained.

title: Solid engineering fundamentals
review: The syllabus is autonomous and frequently updated. Practical sessions are taken very seriously.

title: Hard to get a hostel seat
review: The hostel merit cutoff is very high. Infrastructure is average, but living outside is also affordable.

title: Experienced but traditional
review: Most of the senior faculty are incredibly knowledgeable, though their teaching style can be very old-school.

title: MindSpark is incredible
review: The technical fest MindSpark is extremely well-organized. The robotics and satellite clubs are nationally recognized.

title: Unbeatable ROI in Maharashtra
review: With very low government fees and average packages matching top NITs, it is a steal.

title: Unmatched research ecosystem
review: The best place in India for pure science and research. The facilities and global collaborations are outstanding.

title: Deep conceptual focus
review: The focus is completely on understanding the core concepts rather than just passing exams. Very rigorous.

title: Good for R&D, not typical IT
review: If you want R&D roles at top tech companies, it's great. Not the place if you just want a standard software engineering job.

title: Quiet, lush green campus
review: The campus is literally a forest in the middle of Bangalore. Extremely peaceful, though less vibrant culturally than IITs.

title: Decent accommodation
review: Rooms are single for postgrads and basic. The mess food is highly varied given the diverse student body.

title: World-class scientists
review: You are taught by pioneers in their respective fields. They are demanding but extremely inspiring.

title: A bit slow
review: Administrative processes regarding fellowships and grants can sometimes take a long time.

title: Highly subsidized
review: The fees are very low, and almost everyone gets a fellowship or stipend. Unbelievable value.

title: Private beach!
review: Having a private beach on campus is an absolute flex. The sunset views are a great stress buster.

title: Top-tier IT placements
review: CSE and IT branches have near 100% placement with stellar packages. Core companies also visit in good numbers.

title: Standard but solid curriculum
review: The coursework is standard for NITs, but peer learning and active coding culture elevate the academic environment.

title: Good mix of old and new
review: The new lecture hall complexes are great, though some of the older departmental buildings show their age.

title: Mega hostels are great, older ones aren't
review: If you get the newer mega hostels, it's amazing. Food is mostly South Indian, which takes time to adjust to for North Indians.

title: Helpful but strict
review: Most professors are very approachable, but they are very strict regarding the 75% attendance rule.

title: Incident is huge
review: Incident is one of the best cultural fests in South India. Engineer (tech fest) is also very competitive and fun.

title: Sometimes inefficient
review: Administrative work like fee waivers or transcript requests can be unusually slow.

title: Rigorous case study method
review: The academic pressure is immense. The case study pedagogy forces you to think like a CXO from day one.

title: The absolute best
review: Top consulting (MBB), private equity, and investment banking firms hire extensively here. Packages are unparalleled.

title: Hectic but rewarding
review: You will survive on 4 hours of sleep, but the bonding with your study groups and batchmates is for a lifetime.

title: Heritage red brick campus
review: The Louis Kahn architecture is iconic. Classrooms are state-of-the-art and perfectly designed for case discussions.

title: Dorm culture is great
review: Living in dorms is an essential part of the IIMA experience. Facilities are good and well-maintained.

title: Industry leaders
review: Professors are not just academicians; they consult for the government and top MNCs. The insights are brilliant.

title: Expensive but unmatched ROI
review: The fees are very high, but the loan is easily paid off within a few years thanks to the high starting salaries.

title: Professional and efficient
review: The program office is run very professionally. Issues are resolved quickly compared to other institutions.

title: Good for core and IT
review: Great placements for computer science. Core branches like mechanical and civil also get decent opportunities in PSUs and manufacturing.

title: Decent campus vibe
review: The campus is green and peaceful. Surat is a great city to live in, with amazing food and safety.

title: Mess food could be better
review: Hostels are average. The mess food is highly repetitive and not very appetizing after a few months.

title: A bit outdated syllabus
review: The curriculum covers all the basics but lacks integration of the latest technological trends in some branches.

title: Good library and labs
review: The central library is well-stocked. Lab equipment is functional, though some core labs need modern machinery.

title: Mixed experience
review: Some professors are extremely dedicated and helpful, while others are less approachable and strict with grading.

title: Sparsh fest is nice
review: The cultural and technical clubs are quite active. Sparsh is the major highlight of the year.

title: Government fee structure
review: Standard NIT fees with good placement outcomes make it a very safe and solid investment.

title: Very tough grading system
review: Academics here are arguably the toughest among all IITs. The learning curve is steep but very rewarding.

title: Excellent focus on fundamentals
review: The undergraduate research culture is very strong. Many students publish papers in top international journals.

title: Great tech and quant roles
review: High-frequency trading firms and top tech giants recruit heavily. Core placements are also very respectable.

title: Huge campus, lots of cycles
review: The campus is massive and fully residential. Cycling culture is strong, and the environment is very secure.

title: Older halls are cramped
review: While the newer halls are good, the older ones have small rooms and shared amenities that feel dated.

title: Good airstrip and sports
review: Having our own airstrip is unique. The sports complex and computer center are top-notch.

title: Highly knowledgeable
review: The faculty members are absolute experts. They focus a lot on mathematical rigor and theoretical proofs.

title: Standard processes
review: Administrative processes are mostly digitized now, but sometimes approvals can still take a few days.

title: Top consulting and FMCG
review: Placements are stellar, especially for marketing and consulting. 100% placement is a norm here.

title: Intense curriculum
review: The curriculum is very demanding, especially in the first year. Lots of surprise quizzes and case preparations.

title: Beautiful green campus
review: The campus is beautifully landscaped and very green. The night canteen culture is the best part of student life.

title: Decent single rooms
review: Everyone gets a single room. The rooms are basic but functional. The mess food is decent compared to engineering colleges.

title: Well-equipped classrooms
review: Classrooms are modern and built for case discussions. The library is open 24/7 and has a massive collection.

title: Great industry insights
review: Professors bring a wealth of corporate experience. The lectures are highly engaging and interactive.

title: Excellent ROI
review: Though the fee is substantial, the median package easily justifies the cost within a short span of time.

title: Smooth processes
review: Most of the administrative work is student-driven through various committees, making it quite efficient.
